risinfo.chaptertitle: Appendix II. Test Your Knowledge! Answer Key

sect1.title: Chapter 10
sect1.para: 1. b. Nonphysician qualified health care professional is used to describe professionals (eg, licensed clinical psychologists and licensed clinical social workers) who may provide and independently report non-E/M services as allowed by state regulations and payer policy.
2. d. Psychiatric collaborative care management (99492–99494) represents services provided by a team that includes 3 distinct roles: a treating physician or QHP, BHCM, and psychiatric consultant.

3. c. Report testing evaluation services according to the face-to-face time with the patient and the time spent integrating and interpreting data. A minimum of 31 minutes must be provided to report a 1-hour unit of service. Time spent by technicians (eg, in test administration and scoring) is not included.

4. b. Health and behavior assessment and intervention procedures (96156, 96158, 96159, and 96164–96171) are provided by NQHCPs who cannot report E/M services but can independently provide and report assessment and intervention procedures. Physicians and QHPs who can provide and report E/M services do not report these codes.

5. d. Although family members may act as informants during individual psychotherapy, psychotherapy using family psychotherapy techniques is reported with 90846 or 90847 depending on whether the patient is present for the service. When multiple families participate in family psychotherapy at the same session, 90849 is reported once for each family.
